title: Trump’s Indiana Victory Shows His Enduring Grip on Core Republican Supporters
summary: Low approval ratings? MAGA divisions? The president was able to turn out party loyalists in an Indiana primary to help him oust Republican state lawmakers who had crossed him.

title: Iran War Looms Over Vance as He Visits Iowa
summary: The vice president, a skeptic of the war, made his first visit of the cycle to the state that will kick off the 2028 nominating process.

title: Tennessee Republicans Unveil New Congressional Map Carving Up Majority-Black House District
summary: The General Assembly is expected to quickly approve the map, which slices up Memphis, a majority-Black city that makes up most of the state’s lone Democratic district.

title: Iran Is Reviewing a U.S. Peace Proposal
summary: Also, Ted Turner died at 87. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

title: Jeffrey Epstein’s Purported Suicide Note Is Released by Federal Judge
summary: A federal judge released the note on Wednesday, which Jeffrey Epstein’s former cellmate said he found in a graphic novel. The New York Times has not authenticated that Mr. Epstein wrote it.

title: U.S. Troops in Middle East Wait for the Next Big Moment
summary: The U.S. military is essentially on standby as officials give contradictory signals about the status of the Iran war effort.

title: Trump’s Gyrations on the War Leave Even Rubio Out of Sync
summary: The administration’s latest shifts on the status of the conflict show how treacherous it is to speak for a president who cultivates an erratic style.

title: Attack on French Cargo Ship Highlights Continued Risks in Strait of Hormuz
summary: U.S. Central Command and the ship’s owner disagreed on whether the vessel coordinated safe passage with the United States military.

title: Her Self-Experiment with Drug Detox Almost Broke Her
summary: Against expert advice, people are using new and unpredictable synthetic drugs to experiment on themselves in hopes of becoming free of addiction.

title: How the Fight Over Israel Is Playing Out Inside MAGA
summary: The war in Iran has added to a tectonic shift in public opinion — a bipartisan swing away from Israel. Some on the far-right are fighting to keep President Trump’s movement aligned with the Jewish state.

title: Ted Turner, Creator of CNN and the 24-Hour News Cycle, Dies at 87
summary: As one of the most important figures in media history, he oversaw a vast cable empire of news, sports and entertainment channels.

title: Rupert Murdoch, David Zaslav and More Media Moguls Remember Ted Turner, CNN’s Founder
summary: The CNN founder’s death was met with encomiums from leaders in the media industry, which he helped reinvent. “He will be remembered for generations,” said one.

title: Giuliani Seeks to Have Health Care Covered by 9/11 Program
summary: The former New York City mayor, who is improving after a serious case of pneumonia, suffers from a respiratory condition linked to his exposure to toxins at ground zero, his spokesman says.

title: Jack Smith Calls the Justice Dept. ‘Corrupted’ by Trump and His Allies
summary: At a private event in Washington last month, Mr. Smith, the former special counsel, accused Justice Department leaders of targeting people for prosecution to please and impress the president.

title: Suspect Michael Marx Charged With Shooting at Secret Service Agents Near National Mall
summary: The suspect, a 45-year-old from Texas, faces three charges, including assaulting federal officers with a dangerous weapon.

title: Hantavirus Patients Land in Amsterdam With More Cruise Ship Evacuations Planned
summary: Three people with possible symptoms of the disease were evacuated on Wednesday morning from the MV Hondius before it headed to the Canary Islands.

title: Health Authorities Monitor Hantavirus Cruise Passengers in United States
summary: People in at least three states are being monitored after being on the ship. None have shown symptoms, officials said.

title: Should You Worry About Hantavirus?
summary: Here’s what to know about the virus, how it spreads and the risk to the general public.

title: A Landslide in Alaska Set Off a Tsunami. There May Be More to Come.
summary: Scientists say as glaciers retreat in a warming climate, landslide-generated tsunamis are likely to become more frequent.

title: Air-Conditioning Is in Short Supply as Asia Swelters
summary: From classrooms to crowded buses, people across South and Southeast Asia are adjusting to life with less cooling — or none at all — as the war in Iran drives an energy crunch.

title: New Study Shows Risks of Amazon Deforestation. And Rewards of Protection.
summary: Researchers examined the combined effects of tree loss and global warming in an effort to better understand how and when an ecosystem collapse could unfold.

title: The Longevity Secrets Helping Athletes Blow Past the Limits of Age
summary: With cutting-edge sports medicine and sci-fi gadgetry, more and more athletes are figuring out how to extend their careers.

title: A Hotel in Venezuela’s Capital Has Become the U.S. Embassy’s De Facto Headquarters
summary: The J.W. Marriott in Venezuela’s capital is suddenly awash in U.S. diplomats, spies and fortune seekers, showcasing changes in the country.

title: In ‘Rocky Horror,’ Luke Evans Finds His Ballad of Sexual Liberation
summary: What does it take to play Frank-N-Furter in “The Rocky Horror Show” on Broadway? Luke Evans transforms in five-inch heels and an endless supply of glitter.

title: The New El Niño Might Make 2027 the Hottest Year on Record
summary: The El Niño brewing this year might make 2027 the hottest year on record.

title: Neil deGrasse Tyson: Give Us the Aliens
summary: Is that too much to ask for?

title: The Boxing Match That Killed a Police Recruit
summary: A Massachusetts State Police cadet, already hobbled from a previous bout, raised his hand to take on a more skilled fighter, and investigators say no one stopped him. Four people face charges.

title: Elon Musk’s Confidante Shivon Zilis Is Cast as His Inside Source at OpenAI
summary: Shivon Zilis worked closely with Elon Musk while she was on OpenAI’s board. Her ties to the world’s richest man were detailed in a landmark trial on Wednesday.

title: Anthropic’s C.E.O. Says It Could Grow by 80 Times This Year
summary: The chief executive, Dario Amodei, said the rapid growth had exponentially increased the start-up’s need for more computing power.

title: Crossing Guard Is Fatally Hit by Car in New Jersey While Escorting Children
summary: Jean Schultz, 80, had been a crossing guard in Woodland Park since 2022. She died after she was struck by a vehicle on Monday, the authorities said.
